sections.title: What belongs here
sections.summary: RPGs, roguelites, loot/progression games, and ability-driven games where content volume, balance tuning, and systemic progression matter more than authored one-off scripts.

sections.title: Core structural rule

sections.title: Preferred project structure
sections.summary: - canonical content data - validation and codegen layer where needed - simulation and balance tooling - combat/effect runtime - progression and inventory runtime - client/presentat

sections.title: Source-of-truth boundaries
sections.summary: Source-of-truth data should usually include: - items, abilities, enemies, drops, and formulas - stat curves and progression unlocks - content gating and environment flags - tuning

sections.title: Verification model

sections.title: Release and hotfix concerns

sections.title: Failure modes
sections.summary: - content data edited in multiple inconsistent places - opaque formulas nobody can reason about - ability logic duplicated between client and server - live tuning with no historica
